title: Pepperoncini White Bean Stew
cScaleSummary: C1 because the only meaningful cholesterol contributor is the beef stock. The white beans, mushrooms, barley, olive oil, onion, garlic, pepperoncini peppers, brine, herbs, and bread do not contribute cholesterol, so the per-serving cholesterol remains low.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the stew is built around white beans, barley, mushrooms, and aromatics, making it legume-forward and fiber-forward. Flavor is developed through browning, pepperoncini acidity, herbs, and broth rather than heavy animal fat.
intro: Pepperoncini braises became popular in American home kitchens because the peppers and their brine deliver salt, acidity, and gentle heat in one ingredient. Many versions rely on fatty cuts of beef cooked slowly in a crockpot. This stew keeps the same garlic and pepperoncini backbone but builds the body with mushrooms, white beans, and pearled barley. The result is hearty, rustic, and bright instead of heavy, with a broth designed to be soaked up with hot crusty bread.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Beans
items: Soak the dried white beans overnight in plenty of water. Drain, cover with fresh water, and simmer about 60 to 90 minutes until tender. Drain and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the Aromatic Base
items: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly golden. Add the whole garlic cloves and cook 3 to 4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned in spots.

stepGroups.title: Build the Stew
items: Add the sliced mushrooms, kosher salt, and black pepper. Let them cook undisturbed for about 3 to 4 minutes so they release moisture and begin browning. Stir and continue cooking another 5 to 6 minutes until most of the moisture evaporates and the mushrooms develop good color.
Add the dry pearled barley and cook about 2 minutes, stirring frequently to lightly toast the grains.

Add the pepperoncini peppers, ½ cup of the pepperoncini brine, the beef stock, rosemary sprig, and chopped oregano. Scrape the bottom of the pot firmly with a spoon to release any browned bits. Bring the stew to a gentle simmer. Reduce the heat to low and cook uncovered for about 25 minutes so the barley begins to soften and hydrate.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Stew
items: Stir in the cooked white beans and continue simmering about 20 minutes more, until the barley is tender, the broth thickens slightly, and the garlic cloves have become soft. Stir in the remaining ½ cup pepperoncini brine and simmer another 5 minutes. Press several of the softened garlic cloves against the side of the pot with the back of a spoon so they dissolve slightly into the broth and give it a naturally creamy body. Remove the rosemary sprig and stir in the chopped parsley.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Serve in wide bowls with thick slices of hot crusty bread. Spread the softened garlic cloves onto the bread and use it to soak up the pepperoncini broth.
